name: Random Forest Classifier
description: Random Forest is an ensemble-based classification algorithm that builds multiple decision trees and combines their outputs to improve prediction accuracy and stability across both binary and multiclass problems.
input: Takes in a DataFrame and performs Random Forest Classification
output: Random Forest Classification Model generated is passed along to the next nodes. The input DataFrame is also passed along to the next nodes

fields.name: featuresCol
fields.title: Features Column
fields.description: Vector column containing all input features used by the forest of trees to learn classification patterns.

fields.name: labelCol
fields.title: Label Column
fields.description: Target column representing the actual business outcome or class the model is trained to predict.

fields.name: predictionCol
fields.title: Prediction Column
fields.description: Name of the column where predicted class values are written after model scoring.

fields.name: splitRatio
fields.title: Split Ratio
fields.description: Controls how the dataset is divided between training and validation to assess model performance.

fields.name: featureSubsetStrategy
fields.title: Feature Subset Strategy
fields.description: Determines how many features are randomly selected at each split to reduce correlation between trees.

fields.name: impurity
fields.title: Impurity
fields.description: Metric used to evaluate the quality of splits while growing decision trees.

fields.name: maxBins
fields.title: Max Bins
fields.description: Maximum number of bins used to discretize continuous feature values for split evaluation.

fields.name: maxDepth
fields.title: Max Depth
fields.description: Limits the depth of each decision tree to control complexity and reduce overfitting.

fields.name: minInfoGain
fields.title: Min Information Gain
fields.description: Minimum improvement required before a split is accepted at a tree node.

fields.name: minInstancesPerNode
fields.title: Min Instances Per Node
fields.description: Minimum number of records required in each leaf node to ensure stable and reliable decisions.

fields.name: numTrees
fields.title: Num Trees
fields.description: Total number of decision trees trained in the ensemble. More trees generally improve robustness.

fields.name: subsamplingRate
fields.title: Subsampling Rate
fields.description: Fraction of training data sampled for building each tree, introducing randomness and diversity.

fields.name: seed
fields.title: Seed
fields.description: Seed value used to control randomness and ensure reproducible model results.

fields.name: cacheNodeIds
fields.title: Cache Node Ids
fields.description: Enables caching of intermediate tree nodes to improve training performance.

fields.name: checkpointInterval
fields.title: Checkpoint Interval
fields.description: Defines how frequently training progress is checkpointed to manage memory usage.

fields.name: maxMemoryInMB
fields.title: Max memory
fields.description: Maximum memory allocated for internal histogram and split calculations.

fields.name: minWeightFractionPerNode
fields.title: Min weight fraction per node
fields.description: Ensures each child node retains a minimum proportion of weighted data.

fields.name: bootstrap
fields.title: Bootstrap
fields.description: Determines whether bootstrap samples are used when training individual trees.

fields.name: weightCol
fields.title: Weight Column
fields.description: Optional column defining the relative importance of records during model training.

fields.name: minInfoGainGrid
fields.title: Min Information Gain Param Grid Search
fields.description: Candidate minimum information gain values evaluated during hyperparameter tuning.

fields.name: maxBinsGrid
fields.title: Max Bins Param Grid Search
fields.description: Alternative Max Bins values tested to optimize split precision.

fields.name: maxDepthGrid
fields.title: Max Depth Param Grid Search
fields.description: Different tree depth values evaluated to balance accuracy and interpretability.

fields.name: numTreesGrid
fields.title: Number trees Param Grid Search
fields.description: Different numbers of trees evaluated to improve ensemble stability.

fields.name: output_confusion_matrix_chart
fields.title: Output Confusion Matrix Chart
fields.description: Enables visualization of prediction accuracy across classes.

fields.name: cm_chart_title
fields.title: Confusion Matrix Chart Title
fields.description: Title displayed on the confusion matrix visualization.

fields.name: cm_chart_description
fields.value: Visual Representation of Predicted vs. Actual Classes
fields.title: Confusion Matrix Chart Description
fields.description: Business explanation of how predicted outcomes compare to actual results.

fields.name: confusionMatrixTargetLegend
fields.title: Confusion Matrix Target Legend
fields.description: Legend label representing actual class values.

fields.name: confusionMatrixPredictedLabelLegend
fields.title: Confusion Matrix PredictedLabel Legend
fields.description: Legend label representing predicted class values.

fields.name: confusionMatrixCountLegend
fields.title: Confusion Matrix Count Legend
fields.description: Legend label representing record counts for each outcome.

fields.name: confusionMatrixRowDescription
fields.title: Confusion Matrix Outcome description
fields.description: Business interpretation of correct predictions and misclassifications.

fields.name: output_roc_curve
fields.title: Output ROC Curve
fields.description: Enables ROC curve visualization to assess classification performance.

fields.name: roc_title
fields.title: ROC Curve Chart Title
fields.description: Title displayed on the ROC curve chart.

fields.name: roc_description
fields.title: ROC Curve Chart Description
fields.description: Explanation of how well the model distinguishes between different classes.

fields.name: xlabel
fields.title: X Label
fields.description: X-axis representing the false positive rate.

fields.name: ylabel
fields.title: Y Label
fields.description: Y-axis representing the true positive rate.
